operating_state: ConocoPhillips completed the $16.5 billion Marathon Oil acquisition in November 2024, issuing 143 million shares and assuming $4.7 billion in debt, adding Lower 48 unconventional assets. Total production increased 161 MBOED to 1,987 MBOED with Marathon contributing 334 MBOED in December and record Lower 48 production of 1,152 MBOED. Worldwide realized price decreased six percent to $54.83 per BOE from lower crude and natural gas prices, with WTI down two percent to $75.72 per barrel and Henry Hub natural gas down 17 percent.
cost_structure: Production and operating expenses increased $1.1 billion to $8.8 billion driven by higher lease operating and transportation costs plus Marathon acquisition impact. DD&A increased $1.3 billion to $9.6 billion from higher volumes and elevated rates. Environmental compliance costs totaled $914 million with projected increases to $1.1 billion annually in 2025-2026 from new EPA methane regulations and Inflation Reduction Act charges.
material_events: ConocoPhillips completed the Marathon Oil acquisition for $16.5 billion in November following FTC approval. The company executed strategic debt transactions totaling $5.2 billion in new issuances with $4.1 billion in concurrent repurchases, extending weighted average maturity. The Board increased share repurchase authorization by $20 billion to $65 billion total and increased ordinary dividend 34 percent to $0.78 per share, incorporating prior VROC amount.
macro_exposures: WTI crude oil prices ranged from $87 per barrel in April to $66 per barrel in September, demonstrating continued commodity price volatility with slower global demand growth and higher non-OPEC Plus supplies. Lower 48 natural gas realizations dropped to $0.18 per mcf in third quarter from pipeline capacity constraints before recovering to $0.87 per mcf in fourth quarter. New York and Vermont passed legislation seeking to hold energy companies financially responsible for state climate change mitigation measures.
forward_outlook: Management announced 2025 planned capital return of $10 billion through return of capital framework targeting greater than 30 percent of operating cash flow. Provided 2025 production guidance of 2.34 to 2.38 MMBOED with capital expenditures of approximately $12.9 billion. Management expects approximately $1 billion in synergies from the Marathon Oil acquisition within the first full year and advanced $2 billion disposition target with signed Lower 48 asset sales expected to close in first half 2025.
uncertainty_profile: Purchase obligations total $31.6 billion including transportation capacity and commodity purchases. Integration challenges from the Marathon Oil acquisition may result in unforeseen expenses and operational disruptions. Cities, counties and governments filed climate change lawsuits seeking unspecified compensatory damages with additional similar lawsuits expected. The company maintains CERCLA potentially responsible party sites with uncertain remediation timing and costs extending decades.
